title: Auto Mechanic
tagline: Diagnose, estimate, and repair — with Spanish-speaking customers and crews.
chapterIntro: As an auto mechanic, clear communication with Spanish-speaking customers is essential for safety, trust, and business. This chapter teaches you how to explain vehicle problems, present estimates, describe repairs, and authorize work—all in practical, customer-focused Spanish.

grammarFocus.title: Explaining Problems & Giving Estimates (Present Tense)
grammarFocus.explanation: When diagnosing vehicle issues and presenting estimates, use the present indicative tense to state facts and observations. Use 'necesita' (you need) + object to recommend repairs, and 'está + adjective/condition' to describe current vehicle state. Present tense creates authority and clarity in technical explanations.

examples.english: The engine is running rough because the spark plugs are worn.
examples.spanish: El motor funciona mal porque las bujías están gastadas.
examples.breakdown: está (is) + adjective (running rough) = present state; son (are) = present fact

examples.english: Your brakes need new pads—they are at 15% thickness.
examples.spanish: Sus frenos necesitan pastillas nuevas—están al 15% de espesor.
examples.breakdown: necesitan (need) = present obligation; están (are) = present measurement

examples.english: The transmission is leaking fluid. You need a seal replacement.
examples.spanish: La transmisión pierde líquido. Necesita un reemplazo de sello.
examples.breakdown: pierde (leaks) = present action; necesita (need) = present requirement

phrases.en: I ran a diagnostic and found three issues with your vehicle.
phrases.es: Hice un diagnóstico y encontré tres problemas con su vehículo.
phrases.practice: I ran a diagnostic and found _____ with your vehicle.

phrases.en: Your front brakes are worn—you need new pads and rotors on both sides.
phrases.es: Sus frenos delanteros están gastados—necesita pastillas y rotores nuevos en ambos lados.
phrases.context: describing brake wear and recommending repair
phrases.practice: Your front brakes are _____—you need _____ on both sides.

phrases.en: The transmission is leaking fluid. The repair will cost around $800 in parts and labor.
phrases.es: La transmisión pierde líquido. La reparación costará aproximadamente $800 en refacciones y mano de obra.
phrases.practice: The transmission is _____. The repair will cost around $_____ in parts and labor.

phrases.en: Do you authorize me to replace the spark plugs and air filter today?
phrases.es: ¿Me autoriza a reemplazar las bujías y el filtro de aire hoy?
phrases.practice: Do you authorize me to _____ today?

phrases.en: Your vehicle will be ready at 3 p.m. We will send you a text when it is finished.
phrases.es: Su vehículo estará listo a las 3 p.m. Le enviaremos un mensaje de texto cuando esté terminado.
phrases.practice: Your vehicle will be ready at _____. We will send you a text when it is _____.

phrases.en: The alignment is off by half an inch—this is why your car pulls to the left.
phrases.es: La alineación está desviada por media pulgada—por eso su coche tira hacia la izquierda.
phrases.practice: The alignment is off by _____—this is why your car pulls _____.

phrases.en: This warranty covers parts and labor for two years or 24,000 miles.
phrases.es: Esta garantía cubre refacciones y mano de obra por dos años o 24,000 millas.
phrases.practice: This warranty covers _____ and _____ for two years or 24,000 miles.

phrases.en: Your coolant is low and dirty—I recommend a complete flush and refill.
phrases.es: Su refrigerante está bajo y sucio—le recomiendo un lavado y recarga completos.
phrases.practice: Your coolant is _____ and _____—I recommend a complete flush and _____.

phrases.en: The battery is weak. At 8 years old, it is time to replace it.
phrases.es: La batería está débil. Con 8 años de edad, es hora de reemplazarla.
phrases.practice: The battery is _____. At _____ years old, it is time to _____.

phrases.en: I can order the part today. It will arrive in two business days.
phrases.es: Puedo pedir la pieza hoy. Llegará en dos días hábiles.
phrases.context: managing customer expectations on parts availability
phrases.practice: I can order the part today. It will arrive in _____ days.

phrases.en: Your suspension needs work—the struts are leaking fluid and must be replaced.
phrases.es: Su suspensión necesita trabajo—los amortiguadores están perdiendo líquido y deben ser reemplazados.
phrases.practice: Your suspension needs work—the struts are _____ and must be _____.

phrases.en: We offer a 30-day labor warranty on all repairs we perform.
phrases.es: Ofrecemos una garantía de 30 días en la mano de obra para todas las reparaciones que realizamos.
phrases.practice: We offer a _____ warranty on all repairs we _____.

fillInTheBlank.english: The engine is _____ because the oil is dirty.
fillInTheBlank.spanish: El motor _____ mal porque el aceite está sucio.
fillInTheBlank.hint: Use present tense to describe current engine condition.

fillInTheBlank.answer: new pads and rotors / pastillas nuevas y rotores
fillInTheBlank.hint: Think of the two main components of a modern brake system.

fillInTheBlank.english: The transmission is _____. You need a _____ replacement.
fillInTheBlank.spanish: La transmisión _____. Necesita un reemplazo de _____.
fillInTheBlank.hint: What prevents transmission fluid from escaping?

fillInTheBlank.english: Your vehicle will be ready at _____. We will send you a _____.
fillInTheBlank.spanish: Su vehículo estará listo a las _____. Le enviaremos un _____.
fillInTheBlank.answer: 3 p.m. / text message / las 3 p.m. / mensaje de texto
fillInTheBlank.hint: How do shops communicate completion time with modern customers?

fillInTheBlank.english: This _____ covers parts and labor for two years.
fillInTheBlank.spanish: Esta _____ cubre refacciones y mano de obra por dos años.
fillInTheBlank.hint: What do shops offer to protect the customer after repair?

translate.spanish: Sus neumáticos están gastados. Necesita reemplazarlos.
translate.english: Your _____ are worn. You need to _____.

translate.spanish: La alineación está desviada. Por eso el coche tira hacia la izquierda.
translate.english: The _____ is off. That is why the car pulls to the _____.

translate.spanish: El diagnóstico reveló problemas con el motor y la transmisión.
translate.english: The _____ revealed problems with the _____ and the _____.

translate.spanish: ¿Me autoriza a hacer esta reparación hoy?
translate.english: Do you _____ me to _____ this repair today?

culturalNote.title: Building Trust in Bilingual Auto Shops
culturalNote.content: In Spanish-speaking communities, a mechanic who speaks Spanish is not just convenient—it is a trust marker. Customers appreciate clear, honest explanations in their primary language because auto repair is expensive and often involves complex technical decisions. Regional terminology varies significantly: in Mexico and Central America, 'refacciones' is standard; in Spain and Argentina, 'repuestos' is preferred. For tires, 'llantas' is common in Latin America, while 'neumáticos' is standard in Spain. Using a customer's preferred regional term (when you know it) signals respect and familiarity. Many Spanish-speaking mechanics build loyal customer bases by offering multi-language service bulletins, translating warranty documents, and explaining estimates in person—not just on paper. This chapter emphasizes present-tense diagnosis because authoritative, clear explanations ('Su coche necesita...') build confidence in technical expertise. Always ask customers their preferred terminology and adjust accordingly.
